title: Safety-guided KV autotuner and key ontology for cost-aware caching
preview: Goal: deliver an automated recommendation and safe-apply system that proposes per-prefix/per-key TTLs, batching knobs, and cache-key reshaping suggestions with a probabilistic hit-rate model and gated rollout controls. The feature should reduce token cost for common workloads by ~10–30% without increasing tail latency or error surface. This ticket covers modeling, recommendation heuristics, safety gates (shadow/gradual apply/auto-rollback), and a minimal console integration to present suggested changes and explain expected cost/latency trade-offs.
text: Goal: deliver an automated recommendation and safe-apply system that proposes per-prefix/per-key TTLs, batching knobs, and cache-key reshaping suggestions with a probabilistic hit-rate model and gated rollout controls. The feature should reduce token cost for common workloads by ~10–30% without increasing tail latency or error surface. This ticket covers modeling, recommendation heuristics, safety gates (shadow/gradual apply/auto-rollback), and a minimal console integration to present suggested changes and explain expected cost/latency trade-offs.

title: Automated batching & caching optimizer with safe rollout controller
preview:
Goal: Build an automated recommendation and apply pipeline that adjusts batching and prefix/KV cache parameters to reduce per-token cost while preserving latency and throughput SLOs. The system must be able to propose parameter sets, apply them progressively (canary -> ramp -> full), detect anomalies (regressions in p50/p95/p99 latency, queueing, dropped requests), and rollback safely with minimal disruption.

Scope: optimizer engine for live traffic that suggests and (optionally) applies changes to: target batch size distributions, max concurrent batches, prefix/KV cache TTLs and eviction policies, and model variant routing weights (quantized vs full). Integrations: Redwood Console (observability hooks and rollout UI), Runtime control plane (apply config + safe rollback API), Optimize recommendations console, and alerting (PagerDuty/SLO-driven alerts).

Non-goals: replacing manual SLO tuning; offline-only tuning; model training changes. This work focuses on inference-time config and routing changes only.

Non-goals: replacing manual SLO tuning; offline-only tuning; model training changes. This work focuses on inference-time config and routing changes only.

title: Reserved pool allocation stall under high-tenant-cardinality causing sustained shortfall
preview: During a high-traffic window we observed a sustained shortfall in reserved capacity for several customers using Dedicated pools. The allocator appeared healthy but headroom accounting stopped increasing for affected pools, causing the autoscaler to avoid scaling up and customers to see elevated throttles and tail latency. This ticket covers investigation, emergency mitigation, hotfix rollout, and follow-up actions.
text: During a high-traffic window we observed a sustained shortfall in reserved capacity for several customers using Dedicated pools. The allocator appeared healthy but headroom accounting stopped increasing for affected pools, causing the autoscaler to avoid scaling up and customers to see elevated throttles and tail latency. This ticket covers investigation, emergency mitigation, hotfix rollout, and follow-up actions.

title: Phase-gated launch corridor and regional ramp playbook for runtime/console/api
preview: Create an operational playbook and calendar pattern that coordinates a phase-gated launch corridor across the Runtime, Console, and API surfaces. The purpose is to (1) reduce cross-team ambiguity during canary/capacity ramp, (2) define regional ramp order (NA -> EU -> APAC) with explicit SLA smoke windows, and (3) give step-by-step gating criteria tied to telemetry, cost, and security checks. This ticket captures the decision record, runbook steps, stakeholder owners, and the calendar template to be reused for future model/runtime releases.
text: Create an operational playbook and calendar pattern that coordinates a phase-gated launch corridor across the Runtime, Console, and API surfaces. The purpose is to (1) reduce cross-team ambiguity during canary/capacity ramp, (2) define regional ramp order (NA -> EU -> APAC) with explicit SLA smoke windows, and (3) give step-by-step gating criteria tied to telemetry, cost, and security checks. This ticket captures the decision record, runbook steps, stakeholder owners, and the calendar template to be reused for future model/runtime releases.

title: Operator Inkling: taskization pipeline for one-click remediations
preview: Introduce 'Operator Inkling' — a lightweight suggestion + taskifier that converts contextual diagnostic signals into pre-validated runbook tasks (runlets) for fast operator remediation. The goal is not to fully automate risky fixes, but to reduce cognitive load and time-to-remediate by surfacing a validated, parameterized task that an operator can review and execute with one click. This ticket builds the end-to-end pipeline: suggestion heuristics, runlet template selection, permission checks and audit logging, dry-run & simulation, and a controlled canary rollout in the Console and ops CLI.
text: Introduce 'Operator Inkling' — a lightweight suggestion + taskifier that converts contextual diagnostic signals into pre-validated runbook tasks (runlets) for fast operator remediation. The goal is not to fully automate risky fixes, but to reduce cognitive load and time-to-remediate by surfacing a validated, parameterized task that an operator can review and execute with one click. This ticket builds the end-to-end pipeline: suggestion heuristics, runlet template selection, permission checks and audit logging, dry-run & simulation, and a controlled canary rollout in the Console and ops CLI.

title: Model/Runtime Affinity: Pinning Strategy, Grace Periods, and Progressive Rollback Tooling
preview:
Problem statement:
Customers and internal tenants sometimes pin a model version that depends on runtime behavior (serialization, KV cache format, kernel selection). When we roll runtime or model versions independently we risk silent correctness regressions or hard-to-observe errors (e.g., tokenization drift, cache corruption, attention kernel mismatches). Current routing falls back to the latest compatible model variant but lacks a formal affinity/pinning model, grace window, or a deterministic rollback plan.

Goal:
Define and implement a model/runtime affinity system that: (1) allows fine-grained pinning at route/customer/model-level, (2) exposes LTS channels and grace periods for runtime upgrades, (3) provides automated canary gates and rollback triggers, and (4) implements deterministic fallback policies across minor/major version boundaries. The design should avoid large manual cutovers and make upgrade behavior auditable.

Scope of this ticket:
- Design the affinity semantics and storage (how pins are expressed in routing policies).
- Implement routing-layer checks that consult a compatibility index before sending traffic to a runtime/model pairing.
- Add rollout gates: canary percentage, health thresholds (latency/5xx/regression detection), and automatic rollback triggers (severity, time window).
- Introduce a configurable grace period for model-runtime mismatches to allow background migrations (e.g., KV cache warming).
- Provide operator tooling: rollback button, migration preview, and a compatibility matrix UI endpoint for Console/CLI.

Non-goals:
- This ticket does not implement full automated model retraining or automatic patching of model artifacts. It focuses on routing, gates, and rollback tooling for serving infrastructure.

title: Rolling SLI Census & Degradation Credit Orchestrator for Per-Endpoint Model Contracts
preview: Goal: Implement a rolling SLI census and a degradation-credit orchestrator that automates error-budget allocation and temporary crediting across endpoints and model variants during multi-region failover and circuit-breaker-driven load-shedding. This ticket defines the API surface, telemetry requirements, decision heuristics, and integration points with Console, routing layer, and rollout guardrails. We aim to reduce manual rescue operations during regional pressure by enabling programmatic redistribution of degradation allowance while preserving service contracts for high-priority routes.
text: Goal: Implement a rolling SLI census and a degradation-credit orchestrator that automates error-budget allocation and temporary crediting across endpoints and model variants during multi-region failover and circuit-breaker-driven load-shedding. This ticket defines the API surface, telemetry requirements, decision heuristics, and integration points with Console, routing layer, and rollout guardrails. We aim to reduce manual rescue operations during regional pressure by enabling programmatic redistribution of degradation allowance while preserving service contracts for high-priority routes.

title: Archiveable quant bundles with immutable hash index and safety pilots
preview: Create an archiveable artifact format and operational flow for quantization outputs (FP8/INT8 calibration profiles, factor tables, per-layer statistics) that are: (1) content-addressable and signed; (2) discoverable via an immutable hash index; and (3) safe to rehydrate into running runtimes with automated pre-apply validation checks. Goal is to enable reproducible rollbacks and reproducible cross-region reruns of calibration pipelines while minimizing storage growth and avoiding silent accuracy regressions.
text: Create an archiveable artifact format and operational flow for quantization outputs (FP8/INT8 calibration profiles, factor tables, per-layer statistics) that are: (1) content-addressable and signed; (2) discoverable via an immutable hash index; and (3) safe to rehydrate into running runtimes with automated pre-apply validation checks. Goal is to enable reproducible rollbacks and reproducible cross-region reruns of calibration pipelines while minimizing storage growth and avoiding silent accuracy regressions.

title: Incident retro & action plan for RBACv2 audit-log regression and SAML session anomalies
preview: Summary: On 2025-02-15 we observed a ~45 minute window where RBAC v2 change events were not consistently emitted to the central audit stream and several SAML session refresh events were dropped by the audit pipeline. Symptoms included missing admin role-change entries in the audit dashboard, delayed session invalidation notices, and a spike in console-facing 403s for recently promoted admin users. This ticket captures the RCA, mitigations applied, follow-up engineering work, and verification plan for restoring audit SLOs and hardening admin controls.
text: Summary: On 2025-02-15 we observed a ~45 minute window where RBAC v2 change events were not consistently emitted to the central audit stream and several SAML session refresh events were dropped by the audit pipeline. Symptoms included missing admin role-change entries in the audit dashboard, delayed session invalidation notices, and a spike in console-facing 403s for recently promoted admin users. This ticket captures the RCA, mitigations applied, follow-up engineering work, and verification plan for restoring audit SLOs and hardening admin controls.

title: Modular KV storage adapter and threadpool modernization
preview: Summary: The serving runtime's KV cache layer and I/O threadpool have accumulated brittle assumptions over multiple patches. This task refactors the KV storage surface into a pluggable adapter interface, consolidates shard lifecycle logic, and modernizes the threadpool used by the request scheduler and prefetch workers. Goals are to reduce cross-cutting locks, make on-disk vs in-memory strategies swappable, and upgrade low-level dependencies (liburing/libaio bindings, jemalloc tuning) to improve tail latency and reduce memory fragmentation. The work touches continuous batching (to avoid stalls during cache rehydration), scheduler admission paths, and the async I/O stack.
text: Summary: The serving runtime's KV cache layer and I/O threadpool have accumulated brittle assumptions over multiple patches. This task refactors the KV storage surface into a pluggable adapter interface, consolidates shard lifecycle logic, and modernizes the threadpool used by the request scheduler and prefetch workers. Goals are to reduce cross-cutting locks, make on-disk vs in-memory strategies swappable, and upgrade low-level dependencies (liburing/libaio bindings, jemalloc tuning) to improve tail latency and reduce memory fragmentation. The work touches continuous batching (to avoid stalls during cache rehydration), scheduler admission paths, and the async I/O stack.

title: Fleet velocity attestation harness and customer acceptance playbook
preview: Design and implement an end-to-end harness and playbook to measure and certify dedicated fleet throughput guarantees. Deliver a repeatable benchmarking methodology, telemetry contract, and a customer-facing acceptance checklist for signoff during Dedicated onboards.
text: Design and implement an end-to-end harness and playbook to measure and certify dedicated fleet throughput guarantees. Deliver a repeatable benchmarking methodology, telemetry contract, and a customer-facing acceptance checklist for signoff during Dedicated onboards.

title: Staffing Pulse and Scope Snapshots playbook
preview:
Problem: Mid-cycle surprises and ad-hoc scope cuts are causing inconsistent delivery quality and burnout across teams. We lack a concise, repeatable artifact that lets PMs and Eng leads surface near-term staffing capacity, safe-scope buckets, and quick mitigation actions in one place.

Goal: Create a lightweight playbook and a single-page 'Scope Snapshot' template that teams fill at cycle start and update at weekly staffing pulses. The artifacts should enable: (1) an at-a-glance view of committed vs flexible work, (2) predefined safe-scope bucket rules for quick decommits, (3) a decision ladder for reallocating cross-functional staff without re-planning the whole cycle.

Proposed solution:
- Deliverable A: "Staffing Pulse" cadence definition (roles, signals, owners, meeting script).
- Deliverable B: "Scope Snapshot" template (committed, stretch, de-prioritize, risk notes) in Confluence and as a one-page JSON template for automation.
- Deliverable C: 2-week trial with three squads (Console, Runtime, Dedicated) to validate format and heuristics.

Acceptance criteria:
- Template on Confluence and JSON stored in Drive: teams can produce a Snapshot in 30 minutes.
- Pilot teams reduce unplanned mid-cycle decommits by 50% compared to last cycle baseline.
- Triage time for critical-production work reduced by 20% during pilot.

Stakeholders: Lucas Ortega (PM lead), Maya Chen (Eng lead, Runtime), Samir Patel (Eng lead, Console), Elena Ruiz (Design), Tara McBride (Head of PM).

Measurement: baseline from last three cycles (delivery deviations, number of mid-cycle scope cuts, reassign time). Dashboards: cycle-capacity dashboard (Console) and triage-latency metric (SLO board).

Rollout plan: pilot in 2025-W08–W09 with 3 squads; iterate week-by-week; refactor into org-wide playbook if pilot meets acceptance criteria.

Risks & mitigations:
- Risk: teams treat Snapshot as another doc and don't maintain it. Mitigation: embed Snapshot completion in the cycle checklist and add one Slack reminder hook.
- Risk: definition conflicts with existing tranche policies. Mitigation: coordinate with Capacity Ops (ENG-46228) to align guardrails.

Artifacts & links:
- Confluence template (draft): https://redwood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/PM/pages/Stub/Scope+Snapshot+Template
- Drive JSON template: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-ScopeSnapshotDraft
- Pilot tracker sheet: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1pilot-tracker
- Related GH issue for automation: https://github.com/redwood-inference/cycle-tools/issues/928

title: On-call decision trees, smart throttling, and coordinated failover playflows
preview:
Problem: We continue to receive high-volume P0/P1 paging noise during transient regional overloads. Current alerts fire on raw error rates and queue depth, producing duplicate pages across regions and unnecessary escalations. Existing runbooks are free-text and require on-call judgment, causing slow mitigation and inconsistent failovers.

Goal: Deliver a deterministic on-call decision-tree (playflow) that pairs compact alert signals with automated short-circuit mitigations: coordinated circuit-breaker tripping, regional traffic shedding, and a delayed cross-region failover. Automate the low-risk steps so humans only intervene for escalations. Reduce page volume by 60% for non-actionable events and cut mean time to mitigation (MTTM) for region overloads by 50%.

Scope: Alert rule tuning and tagging, a formal decision-tree document for on-call, automation hooks (server-side throttles and job to flip circuit breakers), dashboard panels for attribution, and a small library of production-safe scripts for automated mitigations. Work includes integrations with PagerDuty, Opsgenie (if present), Console routing controls, and Grafana.

Non-goals: Full self-healing across every failure mode. This ticket focuses on overload and degraded latency events that are predictable by our request/queue/kv-cache signals, not data corruption or security incidents.

title: Cost ancestry: hierarchical cost tracing visualization & CSV export playbook
preview: Design a unified Cost Ancestry view for the Redwood Console that lets users trace spend from high-level product or route down to per-request and per-token line items, visualize attribution paths, and export deterministic CSV / parquet exports for billing and chargeback. The goal is to provide a compact, scannable hierarchy (product -> route -> session/user -> request -> token groups) with rich contextual metadata (model variant, quantization, batch size, dedupe/cache hits, pricing slab) and an exportable schema that maps cleanly to billing units. This ticket captures the prototype, interaction patterns, export schema, and design decisions for PM/Eng review.
text: Design a unified Cost Ancestry view for the Redwood Console that lets users trace spend from high-level product or route down to per-request and per-token line items, visualize attribution paths, and export deterministic CSV / parquet exports for billing and chargeback. The goal is to provide a compact, scannable hierarchy (product -> route -> session/user -> request -> token groups) with rich contextual metadata (model variant, quantization, batch size, dedupe/cache hits, pricing slab) and an exportable schema that maps cleanly to billing units. This ticket captures the prototype, interaction patterns, export schema, and design decisions for PM/Eng review.

title: Overflow absorption policy and deferred-charge metering
preview: Goal: introduce an "overflow absorption" capability for Dedicated capacity that temporarily absorbs short-lived traffic spikes into a soft buffer (absorption credits) and defers explicit overage charges. This feature aims to reduce noisy overage alerts, improve customer experience during transient traffic events, and provide predictable reconciliation for billing. The implementation will add a short-lived credit bucket per tenant, control planes for policy, and reconciliation metering that converts absorbed usage into deferred charges subject to caps and decay rules.
text: Goal: introduce an "overflow absorption" capability for Dedicated capacity that temporarily absorbs short-lived traffic spikes into a soft buffer (absorption credits) and defers explicit overage charges. This feature aims to reduce noisy overage alerts, improve customer experience during transient traffic events, and provide predictable reconciliation for billing. The implementation will add a short-lived credit bucket per tenant, control planes for policy, and reconciliation metering that converts absorbed usage into deferred charges subject to caps and decay rules.

title: Compliance milestone heatmap and dependency matrix for SOC2/ISO rollouts
preview: Purpose: create a single canonical milestone heatmap and dependency matrix that consolidates SOC2 and ISO27001 readiness work (SSO/SAML, audit logging, evidence retention, KMS integration). The deliverable will be a living artifact used by PM, Eng, Security and Sales to track gating criteria, owners, blockers, and mitigation paths across cycles. This ticket coordinates cross-functional owners, surfaces high-risk dependencies, and defines acceptance criteria for gating each compliance milestone.

title: Error Spectrum Lens and Tracehook Consolidation
preview: Deliver an integrated error dashboard (the 'Error Spectrum Lens') and consolidate tracehook behavior so triage teams can quickly attribute failures to timeouts, overload/backpressure, model errors (rejects/invalid outputs), and transport-level codes (HTTP/gRPC). This ticket covers taxonomy definition, metrics/log pipeline changes, tracehook enrichment spec, dashboard panels, and alert-to-SLO mapping. Goal: reduce mean time to root cause for API errors by 40% for P1 incidents and make automated fallback decisions (model variant vs retry) easier to reason about.
text: Deliver an integrated error dashboard (the 'Error Spectrum Lens') and consolidate tracehook behavior so triage teams can quickly attribute failures to timeouts, overload/backpressure, model errors (rejects/invalid outputs), and transport-level codes (HTTP/gRPC). Goal: reduce mean time to root cause for API errors by 40% for P1 incidents and make automated fallback decisions (model variant vs retry) easier to reason about.

title: Telemetry-driven canary controller for Private control plane upgrades
preview: Implement a telemetry-driven Canary Controller and associated Terraform/IaC changes to automate Private (VPC) control plane version rollouts. The controller will use live SLO/health metrics and contract test results to advance, pause, or rollback canary cohorts. This ticket covers design, Terraform module changes for immutable versioned control planes, integration points with the observability pipeline, and a runbook for emergency manual overrides.
text: Implement a telemetry-driven Canary Controller and associated Terraform/IaC changes to automate Private (VPC) control plane version rollouts. The controller will use live SLO/health metrics and contract test results to advance, pause, or rollback canary cohorts. This ticket covers design, Terraform module changes for immutable versioned control planes, integration points with the observability pipeline, and a runbook for emergency manual overrides.

title: deployment-trajectory-heatmap-and-regression-explainer
preview: Build a visual + analytic feature in Console that surfaces the "trajectory" of deployment health over time using a heatmap and automated regression explainer. The feature will help PMs, SREs, and engineers quickly decide whether to continue, pause, or rollback a gradual traffic shift (A/B, canary, shadow) by correlating metric drift with recent changes (model version, routing policy, infra events).

The feature will help PMs, SREs, and engineers quickly decide whether to continue, pause, or rollback a gradual traffic shift (A/B, canary, shadow) by correlating metric drift with recent changes (model version, routing policy, infra events).

title: staged-upgrade-adapter-and-transactional-schema-runner
preview:
Summary: Build a coordinated staged-upgrade adapter paired with a transactional schema runner to enable safe, observable, and reversible upgrades for Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem). This ticket implements the orchestration glue that composes preflight checks, staged rollouts across LTS channels, coordinated DB/schema migration execution with transactional semantics (where possible), and an automated operator-driven rollback path.

Goals: 1) Reduce upgrade failures caused by partial schema changes and race conditions between agents and control plane. 2) Provide a reusable staged-adapter that composes canary -> ramp -> global steps with safepoints and automatic rollback on heuristics. 3) Introduce a "schema-transactionalizer" component that runs safe migrations (DDL/DML) with preflight dry-runs, idempotent guards, and compensating transactions where true transactions are not supported by target DBs. 4) Expose detailed audit events and per-step metrics in Console (upgrade timeline, step latencies, success rates) and support integrations with existing runbooks and incident dashboards.

Scope: - Operator CRD changes for UpgradeRun resources and new UpgradeStage objects. - Agent-side lightweight upgrade-escort that coordinates quiesce, KV cache flush, and model compatibility checks. - Schema-transactionalizer service with drivers for Postgres, MySQL, and SQLite (for on-prem test installs). - Preflight suite: config validation, disk/space checks, backup verification, KMS key verification, and model-variant compatibility. - Rollout gating: semantic version gating, LTS channel mapping, canary percentage scheduling, and traffic shifting. - Observability: structured events to Console, tracing hooks, and rollback reason codification.

Design notes / trade-offs: - Strong preference for transactional migrations on supported DBs to avoid long-lived dual-schema compatibility code, but we will support non-transactional DBs by creating compensating steps and pre-validated idempotent migration plans. Trade-off: longer upgrade windows for non-transactional DBs vs. engineering cost of dual-write compatibility layers.
- We will not attempt to change the data model live for very large tables during the first rollout; instead we offer an operator-configured "deferred background migration" phase coordinated with batch workers.
- Agent-side quiesce will be conservative: short soft-quiesce (stop accepting new inference sessions) then drain to SLO-backed timeouts. This minimizes user impact at cost of slightly increased upgrade latency.

API / CRD sketches: - UpgradeRun { target_version, channel, stages: [preflight, canary, ramp, background-migrations, global], current_stage, status, last_event } - UpgradeStage { name, percentage, timeout_seconds, prehooks, posthooks, migration_plan_id }

Migration plan format: a small declarative JSON that lists ordered steps, each with type: [DDL, DML, backup, verification, reindex], idempotency token, and rollback_compensating_step. Plans are generated by the orchestration service and stored in the control plane (encrypted at rest).

Safety heuristics: - Failure thresholds per stage (5% infra errors OR any data integrity verification failure) trigger automatic rollback to previous version. - Heartbeat checks from agents; missing heartbeat > 2x heartbeat interval transitions the run to Blocked and notifies on-call. - Schema verification includes checksums of critical rows for high-risk tables (configurable).

Testing plan: - Unit tests for migration plan executor and compensating-step generator. - Integration tests in CI with ephemeral Postgres and MySQL containers simulating transactional and non-transactional failures. - Staging runbook dry-runs on private-e2e project with 10% canary traffic for canary validation. - Chaos tests: kill agent mid-stage to ensure rollback path and idempotency.

Telemetry & Runbooks: - Emit structured events: upgrade.stage.start, upgrade.stage.complete, upgrade.stage.failure, migration.step.started, migration.step.completed, migration.step.compensated. - Dashboards: Upgrade Runs list, per-run timeline, and per-cluster success rate. - Runbook link: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/OPS/Private+Upgrade+Runbook (placeholder).

Open questions: 1) Should we require customer opt-in for background migrations on very large on-prem clusters? (proposal: opt-in by default for unattended upgrades; opt-out knob exists) 2) Level of automation for compensating transactions on non-transactional engines vs manual operator review.

Next steps / immediate tasks: - Prototype CRD and controller scaffolding (Diego) - Implement migration plan schema and simple executor (Priya + infra) - Add preflight checks library and integrate with existing preflight service (ENG-56340) - Create initial Console timeline UI contract with PM (link: https://docs.redwood.ai/console/upgrade-timeline - placeholder).
text:
Summary: Build a coordinated staged-upgrade adapter paired with a transactional schema runner to enable safe, observable, and reversible upgrades for Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem). This ticket implements the orchestration glue that composes preflight checks, staged rollouts across LTS channels, coordinated DB/schema migration execution with transactional semantics (where possible), and an automated operator-driven rollback path.

Goals: 1) Reduce upgrade failures caused by partial schema changes and race conditions between agents and control plane. 2) Provide a reusable staged-adapter that composes canary -> ramp -> global steps with safepoints and automatic rollback on heuristics. 3) Introduce a "schema-transactionalizer" component that runs safe migrations (DDL/DML) with preflight dry-runs, idempotent guards, and compensating transactions where true transactions are not supported by target DBs. 4) Expose detailed audit events and per-step metrics in Console (upgrade timeline, step latencies, success rates) and support integrations with existing runbooks and incident dashboards.

Scope: - Operator CRD changes for UpgradeRun resources and new UpgradeStage objects. - Agent-side lightweight upgrade-escort that coordinates quiesce, KV cache flush, and model compatibility checks. - Schema-transactionalizer service with drivers for Postgres, MySQL, and SQLite (for on-prem test installs). - Preflight suite: config validation, disk/space checks, backup verification, KMS key verification, and model-variant compatibility. - Rollout gating: semantic version gating, LTS channel mapping, canary percentage scheduling, and traffic shifting. - Observability: structured events to Console, tracing hooks, and rollback reason codification.

Design notes / trade-offs: - Strong preference for transactional migrations on supported DBs to avoid long-lived dual-schema compatibility code, but we will support non-transactional DBs by creating compensating steps and pre-validated idempotent migration plans. Trade-off: longer upgrade windows for non-transactional DBs vs. engineering cost of dual-write compatibility layers.
- We will not attempt to change the data model live for very large tables during the first rollout; instead we offer an operator-configured "deferred background migration" phase coordinated with batch workers.
- Agent-side quiesce will be conservative: short soft-quiesce (stop accepting new inference sessions) then drain to SLO-backed timeouts. This minimizes user impact at cost of slightly increased upgrade latency.

API / CRD sketches: - UpgradeRun { target_version, channel, stages: [preflight, canary, ramp, background-migrations, global], current_stage, status, last_event } - UpgradeStage { name, percentage, timeout_seconds, prehooks, posthooks, migration_plan_id }

Migration plan format: a small declarative JSON that lists ordered steps, each with type: [DDL, DML, backup, verification, reindex], idempotency token, and rollback_compensating_step. Plans are generated by the orchestration service and stored in the control plane (encrypted at rest).

Safety heuristics: - Failure thresholds per stage (5% infra errors OR any data integrity verification failure) trigger automatic rollback to previous version. - Heartbeat checks from agents; missing heartbeat > 2x heartbeat interval transitions the run to Blocked and notifies on-call. - Schema verification includes checksums of critical rows for high-risk tables (configurable).

Testing plan: - Unit tests for migration plan executor and compensating-step generator. - Integration tests in CI with ephemeral Postgres and MySQL containers simulating transactional and non-transactional failures. - Staging runbook dry-runs on private-e2e project with 10% canary traffic for canary validation. - Chaos tests: kill agent mid-stage to ensure rollback path and idempotency.

Telemetry & Runbooks: - Emit structured events: upgrade.stage.start, upgrade.stage.complete, upgrade.stage.failure, migration.step.started, migration.step.completed, migration.step.compensated. - Dashboards: Upgrade Runs list, per-run timeline, and per-cluster success rate. - Runbook link: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/OPS/Private+Upgrade+Runbook (placeholder).

Open questions: 1) Should we require customer opt-in for background migrations on very large on-prem clusters? (proposal: opt-in by default for unattended upgrades; opt-out knob exists) 2) Level of automation for compensating transactions on non-transactional engines vs manual operator review.

Next steps / immediate tasks: - Prototype CRD and controller scaffolding (Diego) - Implement migration plan schema and simple executor (Priya + infra) - Add preflight checks library and integrate with existing preflight service (ENG-56340) - Create initial Console timeline UI contract with PM (link: https://docs.redwood.ai/console/upgrade-timeline - placeholder).
